description: Letter in the hand of Abū Zikrī, physician to the sultan al-Malik al-ʿAzīz (Saladin's son and successor), sent to his father Eliyyahu the Judge. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: 1193–98 CE, if all the identifications are correct (this document would then be several years earlier than any other document relating to Abū Zikrī or his father Eliyyahu). This is the second page of what was originally a longer letter. Abū Zikrī describes his overwhelming grief upon hearing the news that his younger brother had died. Members of the court came to express their condolences, including the sultan himself, who said that he considers the deceased as equal to his own younger brother, al-Malik al-Amjad. (Information in part from S. D. Goitein, Mediterranean Society, 2:346-47, 5:175–77.) EMS. ASE.
tags: Abu Zikri al-Rayyis, illness: eye, grief, illness: emotion, Illness, illness: respiratory, nar, outburst, illness letter 969-1517, fasting, illness: relapse
description_he: מכתב מאבו זכרי, רופא הסולטן אל-מלך אל-עזיז (בן צלאח-א-דין), לאביו אליהו השופט, 1193–98. מתאר אבלו העמוק על מות אחיו הצעיר; הסולטן עצמו הגיע לנחמו.
